attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~58) — the source note states no age; body weight 110 kg, type 2 diabetes on metformin and a completed 10-day antibiotic course establish an adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as female with NO firm basis in the source note (an abdominal panniculus and intertrigo occur in either sex) — recorded because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected the skin folds, examined the oropharynx, measured vitals/weight and reconciled medications against the EHR. Patient/video-observable findings (beefy-red weeping fold erythema, satellite spots, general distress) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-measured vitals (BP 140/85, HR 92, Temp 37.0, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Wt 110 kg) and the un-palpable early-cellulitis question sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's barrier/decontamination steps were reprojected to patient-performed drying and cream/barrier application.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (candidiasis); the green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (raw, burning, weeping rash in the groin and belly folds, worse after antibiotics, with diabetes) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T2 (prompt in-person GP review), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: candidiasis is a genuine T1-T2 dermatology complaint and the source's own cellulitis red-flag screen is negative and its disposition was topical cream + GP review in 3 days. The diabetes + broken skin justify prompt in-person review over pure self-care and drive prominent mandatory secondary-infection safety-net edges (T4 spreading cellulitis / T5 necrotising infection or sepsis) — but not a baseline uplift. Reflexive escalation to 000/ED and pure self-care with no follow-up are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00210 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis, comorbidities and differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. Several (intertrigo, recent antibiotic exposure, flexural/inverse psoriasis, erythrasma) are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for clotrimazole 1%/hydrocortisone 1% cream and zinc oxide barrier paste were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.
Doses (clotrimazole 1%/hydrocortisone 1% cream twice daily; zinc oxide barrier paste) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
